mechanism_narrative: A downward move in FEDFUNDS transmits through lower discount rates and easier financial conditions, lifting S&P 500 prices over the next month. The signal is confirmed when rate cuts are followed by positive 20-day returns more often than rate hikes or flat policy.
failure_condition: If 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive in a majority of months with declining FEDFUNDS, or rolling 3-year Rank IC falls below 0.03, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A CPIAUCSL inflation acceleration above its recent trend forces tighter policy expectations and compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following month. The effect should be clearest when inflation surprises are broad rather than isolated.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises > 0.4pp annualized above its 6-month trend
failure_condition: Reject if inflation-threshold episodes are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the sign flips to positive for two consecutive rolling years.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE signals weakening household income and slower demand, which reduces expected corporate sales and S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal is strongest when unemployment rises from an already low level because equity markets then reprice recession risk quickly.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3pp over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if unemployment-rise triggers do not precede negative 20-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the average forward return after triggers exceeds the unconditional mean by a statistically meaningful margin.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous improvement in PAYEMS and RSAFS indicates stronger payroll income and retail demand, which supports earnings expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal requires both series to improve together; one without the other is not sufficient.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint-improvement condition does not outperform either series alone, or if triggered months show negative 20-day SPX returns in at least 55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: An upturn in INDPRO combined with rising HOUST indicates stronger real activity and construction demand, supporting cyclical earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The mechanism is a growth-confirmation trade: production and housing must both improve for the signal to hold.
failure_condition: Reject if periods with concurrent INDPRO and HOUST improvement fail to produce positive 60-day SPX returns more than 55% of the time, or if the interaction term adds no incremental predictive power.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in GDPC1 growth signals slowing aggregate demand and weaker future earnings, which depresses S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The signal is most reliable when the GDP release confirms a broad slowdown rather than a one-off inventory effect.
failure_condition: Reject if low-GDP-growth episodes are not followed by weaker 60-day SPX returns than baseline, or if the sign remains negative in less than 40% of triggered cases over any 4-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A low and stable UNRATE combined with continued PAYEMS growth supports risk appetite through resilient income growth and limited recession pricing, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next week. The signal is short-horizon because equity markets react quickly to labor-market resilience.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE below 4.0% and PAYEMS rising over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if low-unemployment plus payroll-growth episodes do not deliver positive 5-day SPX returns more often than 55%, or if the conditional average return is not above the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS stays elevated while CPIAUCSL is still rising, markets infer restrictive real rates and delayed policy relief, which should push S&P 500 returns lower over the next month. The effect requires both tight policy and sticky inflation; either one alone is insufficient.
failure_condition: Reject if high-rate plus sticky-inflation episodes do not produce negative 20-day SPX returns in a majority of cases, or if the joint condition underperforms a simple rate-only rule.
